heading: Definitions
label: 200102 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)200102–3.16 U.S.C. 460l, , ; , , .Pub. L. 88–29, § 477 Stat. 50Pub. L. 96–205, title VI, § 608(c)94 Stat. 92May 28, 1963Mar. 12, 1980The words “the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands” are omitted as obsolete. See note at 48 U.S.C. prec. 1681. For continued application of certain laws of the United States in certain cases, see the Covenant to Establish a Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands in Political Union with the United States of America ( note), the Compact of Free Association between the Government of the United States of America and the Governments of the Marshall Islands and the Federated States of Micronesia ( note), and the Compact of Free Association between the Government of the United States of America and the Government of Palau ( note). The words “as determined by the Secretary” are added to make it clear that it is the Secretary who determines if it is practicable to include the stated entities.48 U.S.C. 180148 U.S.C. 190148 U.S.C. 1931
Amendments2025— added pars. (1) to (3) and redesignated former pars. (1) and (2) as (4) and (5), respectively.Pub. L. 118–234

children.heading: .—Council
children.label: 1 .—Council
children.text: The term “Council” means the Federal Interagency Council on Outdoor Recreation established under section 200104.

children.heading: .—Federal land and water management agency
children.label: 2 .—Federal land and water management agency
children.text: The term “Federal land and water management agency” means the National Park Service, Bureau of Land Management, United States Fish and Wildlife Service, Bureau of Indian Affairs, Bureau of Reclamation, Forest Service, Corps of Engineers, and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: .—Federal recreational lands and waters
children.label: 3 .—Federal recreational lands and waters
children.text: The term “Federal recreational lands and waters” has the meaning given the term in section 802 of the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act () and also includes Federal lands and waters managed by the Bureau of Indian Affairs, Corps of Engineers, or National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.16 U.S.C. 6801

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 4 .—State
children.text: The term “State”, to the extent practicable, as determined by the Secretary, includes Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Virgin Islands, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: includes the District of Columbia; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent practicable, as determined by the Secretary, includes Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Virgin Islands, and the Northern Mariana Islands.
